issues.summary: slf4j-simple used as sole logging backend for a documented long-running REST server
issues.suggestion: Replace slf4j-simple with logback-classic (add logback-classic to pom.xml as runtime scope and remove slf4j-simple). Add a logback.xml configuration file with a rolling file appender for server mode.
evidence: pom.xml: <dependency>slf4j-simple</dependency> with <scope>runtime</scope> — no logback or log4j2 binding
README and CLAUDE.md describe 'server' mode as a primary use case: 'java -jar concept-sketch.jar server --index data/index/ --port 8080'
slf4j-simple writes to System.err only, with no file rotation, no async output, and no external configuration

issues.summary: API handler methods each mix parameter parsing, validation, query execution, and response building in 70-120 line monolithic methods
issues.suggestion: Extract each concern into a named private method: parseExploreParams(), validateRelation(), buildSeedCollocatesSection(), buildEdgesSection(), etc. Each handler method then reads as an orchestration of ~6 lines. This also makes the response structure explicit without requiring typed DTOs.
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore: ~120 lines, performs parameter extraction (8 params), grammar config lookup, pattern derivation, executeSurfacePattern call, and builds 5 separate response sections (parameters, seedCollocs, nouns, coreCollocs, edges)
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti: ~100 lines with the same structure; the intersection loop is embedded inline
handleSemanticField: ~70 lines of adjective-profile list building with nested category logic inline

issues.summary: CQL pattern strings used for collocate filtering are hardcoded inline in handler logic without named constants
issues.suggestion: Use the existing SemanticFieldExplorer.ADJECTIVE_PATTERN and NOUN_PATTERN constants (make them package-accessible or move them to a shared constants class) instead of repeating the literal strings in the handler.
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore line: String simplePattern = relationConfig.get().collocatePosGroup() == PosGroup.ADJ ? "[xpos=\"JJ.*\"]" : "[xpos=\"NN.*|VB.*\"]";
The same xpos pattern strings appear in SemanticFieldExplorer as private static final constants (ADJECTIVE_PATTERN, NOUN_PATTERN) but are not reused here

issues.summary: CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens uses indexOf(']') without depth tracking, silently failing on nested brackets
issues.suggestion: Replace the indexOf(']') scan with a depth-counting loop (depth++/depth-- on '['/']'), then document in the Javadoc that quoted strings containing brackets are not supported. Alternatively, add a Javadoc note explicitly stating the no-nesting assumption so future editors are warned.
evidence: CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens: int end = pattern.indexOf(']', i); — no nesting depth counter, so a pattern containing nested brackets (e.g. character classes or future grammar extensions) would split incorrectly
The Javadoc does not document this limitation

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser static compiled Pattern fields lack private modifier, leaking implementation details
issues.suggestion: Add private modifier to all static Pattern fields in BlackLabSnippetParser. They are not used by any other class in the package.
evidence: static final java.util.regex.Pattern LEMMA_ATTR = ...; — package-accessible, not private
Same for XPOS_ATTR, UPOS_ATTR, SENT_BOUND_LEFT, SENT_BOUND_RIGHT, XML_SENT_OPEN, XML_SENT_CLOSE, LEMMA_ATTR_ANY — 7 pattern constants all package-accessible
These patterns are implementation details of the parser and should not be part of the package API

issues.summary: API layer imports six SemanticFieldExplorer inner classes, coupling api to query internals
issues.suggestion: Move ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExploreOptions, and Edge to a new pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.model package. Update all imports in ExplorationHandlers and SemanticFieldExplorer. This removes the direct api→query.SemanticFieldExplorer inner-class dependency.
evidence: ExplorationHandlers.java lines 10-16: 'import pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.query.SemanticFieldExplorer.ComparisonResult; import ...AdjectiveProfile; import ...ExplorationResult; import ...DiscoveredNoun; import ...CoreCollocate; import ...ExploreOptions;'
TODO(42e2a9a1) acknowledges: 'These imports bind the API layer to SEF's inner result classes. Once SEF inner classes are moved to a dedicated model/ package, update these imports.'

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti duplicates multi-seed orchestration instead of delegating to SemanticFieldExplorer
issues.suggestion: Add a SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreMulti(Set<String> seeds, String bcqlPatternTemplate, ExploreOptions opts) method that encapsulates the per-seed loop, intersection computation, and edge building. ExplorationHandlers.handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti should call this single method and then map the result to a response map.
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti calls executor.executeSurfacePattern directly in a loop, computes set intersection with commonCollocates.retainAll(seedCollocates), and builds edges manually — logic that belongs in SemanticFieldExplorer.
SemanticFieldExplorer already has compareSeeds() and compare() methods for multi-seed comparison; handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti ignores them.

issues.summary: Three handler methods build response Map<String,Object> inline with duplicated rounding and field-naming logic
issues.suggestion: Introduce private static toCollocateMap(WordSketchResult), toDiscoveredNounMap(DiscoveredNoun), toCoreCollocateMap(CoreCollocate), and toEdgeMap(Edge) helper methods (or static factory on the DTOs once they are in model/). This removes the inline serialization noise and makes boundary translation explicit.
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, and handleSemanticField each contain 40-60 lines of 'Map<String,Object> xMap = new HashMap<>(); xMap.put(...)' with identical Math.round(x*100.0)/100.0 rounding patterns for logDice, similarity_score, avg_logdice etc.
No shared DTO or toMap() helper between the three methods.

issues.summary: executeDependencyPattern is public on BlackLabQueryExecutor but absent from QueryExecutor interface
issues.suggestion: Either add executeDependencyPattern to QueryExecutor with a default implementation that ignores headPosConstraint, or consolidate into findDependencyCollocations with an optional constraint parameter. Document the chosen boundary contract on the interface.
evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor exposes executeDependencyPattern(lemma, deprel, headPosConstraint, minLogDice, maxResults) as public but QueryExecutor only declares findDependencyCollocations(lemma, deprel, minLogDice, maxResults).
The richer signature (with headPosConstraint) is inaccessible through the interface, forcing callers to downcast or use the concrete type.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer creates SemanticFieldExplorer internally instead of receiving it via DI
issues.suggestion: Add SemanticFieldExplorer as a constructor parameter to WordSketchApiServer. Update Main.handleServerCommand to construct SemanticFieldExplorer and pass it in. This keeps the construction boundary at Main and makes WordSketchApiServer purely compositional.
evidence: WordSketchApiServer constructor: 'SemanticFieldExplorer semanticFieldExplorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(executor);' — constructed inside, not passed in.
TODO(595bb84c) acknowledges construction complexity but does not address the DI gap for SemanticFieldExplorer.

issues.summary: extractDependentLemmaFromGroup and extractPosFromGroup are private 3-line pass-through methods with no added logic
issues.suggestion: Inline these two methods at their two call sites in executeDependencyPattern. The call sites become 'String lemma = BlackLabSnippetParser.extractCollocateLemma(group.identity().toString())' and 'String pos = BlackLabSnippetParser.extractPosFromMatch(group.identity().toString())' — no abstraction is lost.
evidence: extractDependentLemmaFromGroup: gets identity string from group, calls BlackLabSnippetParser.extractCollocateLemma(identity), returns lowercased result — no validation, no transformation, no added value beyond the single call.
extractPosFromGroup: gets identity string from group, calls BlackLabSnippetParser.extractPosFromMatch(identity), returns result — single line of delegated logic.

issues.summary: Null/empty parameter guard + sendError(400) pattern repeated verbatim 6+ times across handler methods with no shared helper
issues.suggestion: Add a helper to HttpApiUtils: 'static String requireParam(HttpExchange, Map<String,String> params, String key) throws IOException' that checks presence, sends 400 on absence, and returns the value. Handler methods become one line per required parameter and consistently emit the same error format.
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore: 'String seed = params.getOrDefault("seed",""); if (seed.isEmpty()) { HttpApiUtils.sendError(exchange, 400, "Missing required parameter: seed"); return; }'

handleSemanticField: same pattern for 'nouns' parameter. handleSemanticFieldExamples: same for 'adjective' and 'noun'. SketchHandlers.handleConcordanceExamples: same for 'word1' and 'word2'. Pattern is copy-pasted without extraction.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser.extractHeadLemma is a 1-line alias for extractLemmaAt(matchXml, 1) with no added logic
issues.suggestion: If the name adds domain clarity (head vs positional), keep it but document what position 1 represents in the extraction chain. If no caller uses it, remove it. If only one caller exists, inline the call. Avoid 1-line wrappers that add a name but no contract.
evidence: extractHeadLemma: 'static String extractHeadLemma(String matchXml) { return extractLemmaAt(matchXml, 1); }' — the method body is a single delegating call.
Callers that use extractHeadLemma could call extractLemmaAt(xml, 1) directly with no loss of readability.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer retains a deprecated-by-comment indexPath constructor that risks double-opening the index
issues.suggestion: Mark SemanticFieldExplorer(String indexPath) with @Deprecated and a Javadoc @deprecated tag pointing to the QueryExecutor constructor. Audit all callers and migrate them to the DI constructor. Once no callers remain, remove it. This eliminates the double-open risk and the ambiguity about which constructor to use.
evidence: Constructor comment: 'Convenience constructor that creates an owned BlackLabQueryExecutor internally. Prefer SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor) (dependency injection) for testability and to avoid double-open of the same index (see issue 297b2b52).'

If any caller uses SemanticFieldExplorer(indexPath) while the server also holds a BlackLabQueryExecutor over the same path, the index is opened twice, which may cause resource contention or memory duplication.

issues.summary: executeDependencyPattern is public on BlackLabQueryExecutor but not declared on QueryExecutor interface
issues.suggestion: Either add executeDependencyPattern to QueryExecutor with an Optional<String> headPosConstraint parameter and a default implementation that delegates to findDependencyCollocations, or merge the functionality by adding an overload of findDependencyCollocations that accepts the constraint. The interface should be the complete API contract.
evidence: QueryExecutor.java declares findDependencyCollocations(lemma, deprel, minLogDice, maxResults) but not executeDependencyPattern(lemma, deprel, headPosConstraint, minLogDice, maxResults).
BlackLabQueryExecutor.executeDependencyPattern is public, suggesting it was intended for callers but was never lifted to the interface.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser.extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition kept alongside its canonical replacement extractLemmaAt without deprecation
issues.suggestion: Add @deprecated to extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition pointing to extractLemmaAt as the canonical replacement. If no test or production code calls extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition directly (verify with grep), make it private or remove it.
evidence: extractLemmaAt Javadoc: 'Extract lemma at a specific 1-based token position from XML snippet. This is the canonical method for positional lemma extraction.'
extractLemmaAt body: 'return extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition(xml, position);' — a 1-line alias.
Both methods are package-visible; callers could use either without guidance.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser.extractCollocateFromMatchText appears unreferenced in production code
issues.suggestion: If extractCollocateFromMatchText has no production callers, remove it from the production class and its corresponding test. If it is kept for completeness, add a Javadoc note explaining when plain-text extraction is needed vs XML extraction.
evidence: extractCollocateFromMatchText(String matchText, int position) extracts a token from a space-separated plain text match by position — a utility that may have been superseded by the XML-based extractors.
No calls to this method found in production source files (api, config, query, utils packages); only BlackLabSnippetParserTest references it in tests.

issues.summary: Seven SemanticFieldExplorer inner result classes should move to a dedicated model/ package to resolve api→query inner-class coupling
issues.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) Create pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.model package. (2) Move ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExploreOptions, Edge to model/ as top-level classes. (3) Update imports in SemanticFieldExplorer and ExplorationHandlers. (4) Verify with: mvn compile (no errors) and mvn test (no regressions). Move order: ExploreOptions first (no dependencies on other DTOs), then the result types, then Edge last (referenced by multiple result types).
evidence: ExplorationHandlers imports: ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ExploreOptions, Edge — all from SemanticFieldExplorer's inner class namespace.
SemanticFieldExplorer Javadoc: 'These should be extracted to a dedicated model/ package in a future refactor (see issue 28bc8918 / a430f585).'
The api package should depend on a model/DTO layer and the query package separately; importing inner classes from a query class conflates the two.

issues.summary: PatternSubstitution belongs in utils, not api — it has no HTTP dependency and provides pure CQL string manipulation
issues.suggestion: Staged reorg: (1) Move PatternSubstitution.java from api/ to utils/. (2) Update the single import in SketchHandlers from api.PatternSubstitution to utils.PatternSubstitution. (3) Verify with: mvn compile && mvn test. No functional changes required — just a package move and import update.
evidence: PatternSubstitution imports: java.util.List, pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.utils.CqlUtils — no HTTP, no HttpExchange, no api-layer type.
Methods substituteCollocate, extractXposFromConstraint, escapeForRegex are pure string operations on CQL patterns — the same concern as CqlUtils.
Its only non-utils caller is SketchHandlers.handleConcordanceExamples in the api package, but its content is CQL utility, not HTTP handling.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer(String indexPath) can create a second BlackLabQueryExecutor over the same index, risking resource contention
issues.suggestion: Mark SemanticFieldExplorer(String indexPath) @Deprecated with a Javadoc @deprecated note. In the constructor body, add a warning log: logger.warn("SemanticFieldExplorer(String) creates an owned index handle; prefer SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor) to share the existing handle."). Audit all callers; if none use it in production, remove it. If removal is deferred, the deprecation marker at least makes the risk visible in IDEs.
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer(String indexPath): 'this.executor = new BlackLabQueryExecutor(indexPath); this.ownsExecutor = true;' — creates a second independent BlackLabIndex handle.

Main.handleServerCommand creates BlackLabQueryExecutor(indexPath) explicitly, then passes it to WordSketchApiServer which calls new SemanticFieldExplorer(executor) — correct. But SemanticFieldExplorer(String) remains callable.
Code comment: 'Prefer SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor) to avoid double-open of the same index (see issue 297b2b52).' — the risk is documented but not prevented.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor opens the BlackLab index in its constructor, coupling object creation to I/O availability and making the object untestable without a real index
issues.suggestion: Consider a factory method pattern: make the constructor package-private and add a static BlackLabQueryExecutor open(String indexPath) throws IOException factory. This makes the I/O intent explicit in callers ('open' reads as I/O; 'new' does not) and opens the door for a create-uninitialized path useful in testing. As a lower-effort alternative, add a Javadoc note on the constructor: '@throws IOException if the index cannot be opened — this constructor performs file I/O and should not be called in unit tests; use a mock QueryExecutor instead.'
evidence: Constructor body: 'this.blackLabIndex = BlackLab.open(new File(indexPath));' wrapped in try-catch Exception → IOException. BlackLab.open involves file system traversal, Lucene segment loading, and memory mapping.

The QueryExecutor interface is used in tests (SemanticFieldExplorerTest, HybridAlgorithmTest) but BlackLabQueryExecutor itself is only tested via BlackLabDummyTest with a real index path — confirming that the I/O-in-constructor design forces integration tests over unit tests.

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig embeds ~180 lines of BCQL substitution logic that belongs in the pattern layer
issues.suggestion: Move substituteHeadword and mergeLemmaConstraint into PatternSubstitution (or a HeadwordSubstitution helper). Move collocatePosGroup and posGroupFromConstraint into PosGroup enum or CqlUtils. RelationConfig should only hold parsed config fields and delegate to those utilities.
evidence: RelationConfig record (lines ~300–552 of GrammarConfigLoader) contains substituteHeadword(), mergeLemmaConstraint(), extractConstraintAttribute(), escapeForRegex(), collocatePosGroup(), posGroupFromConstraint(), extractLabelContent(), getDeprel() — all query-pattern concerns inside a config record
PatternSubstitution.java already exists in the api package as the designated location for collocate substitution; getFullPattern() should call it rather than duplicating the logic
substituteHeadword has its own hand-rolled CQL bracket-walker (while loop with prefix detection) that is structurally equivalent to CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens but not using it

issues.summary: substituteHeadword in RelationConfig re-implements CQL bracket-walking that CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens already provides
issues.suggestion: Replace the hand-rolled bracket-walker in substituteHeadword with a call to CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens, then process the resulting list. Extend CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens to also return label prefixes if needed, rather than duplicating the scanner.
evidence: GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig.substituteHeadword (lines ~350–410) contains its own while-loop that scans for '[', ']', '"' and handles numbered prefix like '1:' and '2:'
CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens was created specifically as a 'shared primitive' (per its Javadoc) for this bracket-walking; it is imported by PatternSubstitution but not by GrammarConfigLoader
The positions list in substituteHeadword and the tokens list in CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens serve identical purposes

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.compare() fuses profile building, metric computation, sorting, and debug logging into a ~130-line method
issues.suggestion: Extract private helpers: buildRawAdjectiveProfiles(nouns, minLogDice, maxPerNoun) returning Map<String, Map<String, Double>>, and buildAdjectiveProfileList(rawProfiles, nounList) returning List<AdjectiveProfile>. This mirrors the phase separation already done in executeBcqlQuery.
evidence: Phase 1 (lines ~300–325): per-noun adjective lookup filling adjectiveProfiles map
Phase 2 (lines ~329–380): for each adjective computes presentIn, avgScore, maxScore, minScore, variance, commonalityScore, distinctivenessScore — 6 separate metrics
Phase 3 (lines ~382–402): sort by commonality, two separate debug-log blocks (shared, distinctive), then return
Contrast with executeBcqlQuery which correctly decomposes into collectHits() and scoreHits() private methods

issues.summary: Handler methods build identical Map<String, Object> collocations-list shapes inline rather than through a shared formatter
issues.suggestion: Add toResponseMap() to WordSketchResult (or serialize directly with a JSON library). Create a shared private formatCollocations(List<WordSketchResult>) in SketchHandlers. Consider serializing model objects directly via fastjson2's JSON.toJSONString rather than building intermediate maps.
evidence: SketchHandlers.handleFullSketch, handleFullDependencySketch, and handleRelationQueryInternal each contain a nearly identical loop: for (WordSketchResult result : results) { Map<String, Object> word = new HashMap<>(); word.put("lemma", ...); word.put("frequency", ...); word.put("logDice", ...); word.put("pos", ...); }

ExplorationHandlers.handleSemanticFieldExplore builds seedCollocs, nouns, coreCollocs, and edges as separate inline Map constructions — none reused across handleSemanticField which builds a similar edge list
The model classes (WordSketchResult, ExplorationResult, AdjectiveProfile) already exist and could expose toResponseMap() or be serialized directly; the manual map-building is unnecessary indirection

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor has two near-identical dependency query methods that could be unified
issues.suggestion: Deprecate findDependencyCollocations and route its callers through executeDependencyPattern with null headPosConstraint, or add findDependencyCollocations as a one-line delegate: return executeDependencyPattern(lemma, deprel, null, minLogDice, maxResults).
evidence: findDependencyCollocations (line 143): constructs bcql = String.format("\"%s\" -%s-> _", ...), parses pattern, gets headwordFreq, iterates groups with HitPropertyHitText, calls buildAndRankCollocates
executeDependencyPattern (line 372): same construction with optional headPosConstraint variant, same parse/group/buildAndRank flow, additionally extracts POS via extractPosFromGroup
The only structural difference is the optional headPosConstraint; findDependencyCollocations could be replaced by executeDependencyPattern(lemma, deprel, null, minLogDice, maxResults)

issues.summary: ConcordanceResult primary constructor accepts 11 args with 7 nullable fields, creating context-dependent partial objects with no type-level enforcement
issues.suggestion: Introduce a sealed hierarchy: ConcordanceResult (base with sentence/startOffset/endOffset/docId), CollocateResult (extends with collocateLemma/frequency/logDice), SnippetResult (extends with rawXml/lemma/tag/word). Alternatively use two record types and a shared interface. This removes nullable fields and makes the type honest.
evidence: ConcordanceResult Javadoc lists rawXml, lemma, tag, word, docId, collocateLemma as fields that 'may be null depending on how the result was produced'
frequency and logDice are 0 when collocateLemma is null, creating a state where 4 fields (collocateLemma, frequency, logDice + either rawXml or lemma/tag/word) are meaningless
Callers in SketchHandlers check r.getRawXml() != null and r.getCollocateLemma() != null before using those fields — defensive checks that should not be necessary for a well-typed result
forSnippet factory helps but is not enforced: nothing prevents callers from using the 11-arg constructor for snippet results

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor.findCollocations silently degrades to wildcard query on unrecognized cqlPattern format
issues.suggestion: Throw IllegalArgumentException when cqlPattern is not in an expected format, with a descriptive message listing the accepted formats. The warn log can remain but should not be followed by silent execution; callers should fix their patterns rather than receiving degraded results.
evidence: Lines ~88–94: 'Unrecognized cqlPattern format... Falling back to wildcard query' — a logger.warn followed by bcql = String.format("\"%s\" []", lemma) and continued execution

The method is declared as findCollocations(String lemma, String cqlPattern, ...) implying cqlPattern is used; a caller passing an invalid pattern expects either results for that pattern or an error, not silently substituted wildcard results
The fallback produces structurally valid but semantically wrong results (any collocate for lemma rather than the intended pattern-constrained collocates) with no signal to the caller

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader.toJson() will throw NullPointerException when configPath is null (fromReader construction path)
issues.suggestion: Replace configPath.toString() with configPath != null ? configPath.toString() : "<in-memory>" in toJson(). Alternatively, store configPath as an Optional<Path> to make the nullable contract explicit.
evidence: fromReader() factory calls the primary constructor with null as configPath: new GrammarConfigLoader(sb.toString(), null)
toJson() calls configPath.toString() unconditionally: root.put("config_path", configPath.toString())
No null guard exists; calling toJson() on a fromReader()-produced instance will throw NullPointerException with no documentation warning callers of this constraint

issues.summary: HttpApiUtils.parseQueryParams silently drops parameters that fail URL-decoding
issues.suggestion: Either surface decode failures as IllegalArgumentException (and let handlers return 400), or at minimum document the silent-drop behavior on the method's Javadoc so callers know the returned map may be incomplete.
evidence: The catch(Exception e) block inside parseQueryParams logs a warning and calls continue — the failing key-value pair is dropped from the returned map
Callers using params.getOrDefault('key', defaultValue) receive the default value for a dropped parameter, which is indistinguishable from the parameter simply not being provided

Handler code in ExplorationHandlers and SketchHandlers relies on params.getOrDefault with defaults — if a user provides 'top=10' but it fails to decode, the handler silently uses its default, giving no HTTP error

issues.summary: LogDiceCalculator.compute returns 0.0 for both 'not computable' and 'perfect absence', making zero semantically ambiguous
issues.suggestion: Return Double.NaN or a sentinel constant (e.g., LogDiceCalculator.NOT_COMPUTABLE = -1.0) when inputs are invalid (freq <= 0). Callers can then explicitly filter out NOT_COMPUTABLE results vs. treating them as zero-association. Update buildAndRankCollocates to skip NOT_COMPUTABLE entries.
evidence: Returns 0.0 when headwordFreq <= 0 (index lookup failed), when collocateTotal <= 0 (another lookup failure), and when dice <= 0 (co-occurrence is zero or negative)

Callers in BlackLabQueryExecutor.buildAndRankCollocates filter by logDice >= minLogDice — when minLogDice is 0, all 'not computable' entries pass the filter alongside legitimate zero-association entries
There is no way for callers to distinguish 'the index lookup failed so we cannot score this' from 'the collocate genuinely has no association with the headword'

issues.summary: extractLemmaFromMatch and extractCollocateLemma in BlackLabSnippetParser are near-identical methods with only case normalization differing
issues.suggestion: Remove extractLemmaFromMatch and route all callers to extractCollocateLemma (and apply toLowerCase at the call site if needed), or unify both into extractCollocateLemma with a boolean lowercase parameter. Remove extractLemmaFromMatch entirely and update BlackLabQueryExecutor.findCollocations which currently uses it.
evidence: extractLemmaFromMatch (line ~29): iterates LEMMA_ATTR, returns lastLemma.toLowerCase()
extractCollocateLemma (line ~215): iterates LEMMA_ATTR, returns lastLemma (no toLowerCase)
Both return the last lemma match from the XML snippet — the Javadoc on extractCollocateLemma says 'finds the last lemma attribute' which is exactly what extractLemmaFromMatch also does

extractLemmaAt() was also added as a 'canonical method for positional lemma extraction' but extractLemmaFromMatch and extractCollocateLemma persist alongside it, creating three overlapping extraction paths

issues.summary: Second null check on collocateLemma in BlackLabQueryExecutor.findCollocations is unreachable
issues.suggestion: Remove the null check from the second guard: change 'if (collocateLemma == null || collocateLemma.isEmpty())' to 'if (collocateLemma.isEmpty())' after the plain-text extraction branch, making it clear that null is not possible at that point.
evidence: After the branch 'collocateLemma = BlackLabSnippetParser.extractLemmaFromMatch(identity)', the else block assigns: String trimmed = identity.trim(); int lastSpace = trimmed.lastIndexOf(' '); collocateLemma = lastSpace >= 0 ? trimmed.substring(lastSpace + 1) : trimmed;
This assignment always produces a non-null String (trimmed or its substring, both from a String reference)
Immediately after: 'if (collocateLemma == null || collocateLemma.isEmpty()) { continue; }' — the null branch of this condition can never be true after the preceding assignment
Only the isEmpty() check is meaningful here; the null check is dead code that suggests a copy-paste from the earlier null check on extractLemmaFromMatch

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.compare() computes minScore but never uses it in any metric or return value
issues.suggestion: Rename the local variable 'minScore' to 'minLogDice' (or 'minAdjectiveScore') to match the field name it populates in AdjectiveProfile, eliminating the apparent unused-variable confusion.
evidence: Line ~339: double minScore = Arrays.stream(scores).min().orElse(0.0);

minScore is never referenced again in the method — not in variance calculation (which uses avgScore), not in commonalityScore (uses avgScore), not in distinctivenessScore (uses maxScore), and not passed to AdjectiveProfile constructor (which takes avgLogDice, maxLogDice, minLogDice — but those are passed as avgScore, maxScore, minScore respectively)

Wait — AdjectiveProfile constructor does receive minScore as the minLogDice parameter. So minScore IS used — it's passed to the AdjectiveProfile constructor as minLogDice. Re-checking: profiles.add(new AdjectiveProfile(adj, fullScores, presentIn, nounCount, avgScore, maxScore, minScore, variance, commonalityScore, distinctivenessScore)) — minScore IS the 7th argument mapped to minLogDice field
This issue is a false positive on further inspection — minScore is passed to AdjectiveProfile as its minLogDice field. The variable name does not match the field name but the value flows correctly.

issues.summary: RelationConfig.substituteHeadword re-implements CQL bracket-walking that CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens already provides, creating two separate code paths that can diverge
issues.suggestion: Extend CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens to return token blocks including their numeric labels (or add a splitCqlTokensWithLabels variant). Replace the bracket-walker in substituteHeadword and deriveTokenPosition with calls to the shared utility. This is a logic-clarity issue because two walks of the same data structure is harder to reason about than one.
evidence: GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig.substituteHeadword contains a while-loop scanning characters for '[', ']', and numbered prefixes ('1:', '2:'), maintaining a positions list
CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens performs identical bracket-walking (scanning '[', ']', skipping quoted strings), returning a List<String> of token blocks
The GrammarConfigLoader code additionally handles numbered prefixes ('1:[' style) which CqlUtils does not — any bug fix or extension to the CQL bracket-walking logic must be applied in two places
deriveTokenPosition in GrammarConfigLoader also walks the pattern independently, creating a third walk

issues.summary: collectHits initializes 5 variables to empty/null defaults that are always overwritten before use, obscuring the actual flow
issues.suggestion: Extract a private method extractHitData(Concordance conc) that returns either populated values or the empty-string/null defaults, then assign: HitData data = extractHitData(conc); String leftText = data.leftText(); etc. This makes it clear that the empty strings are intentional fallbacks for null concordance, not dead initialization.
evidence: Lines ~261–265: 'String leftText = "", matchText = "", rightText = "", xmlSnippet = ""; String collocateLemma = null;'
All five are reassigned inside 'if (conc != null) { ... }' — the empty-string defaults are only visible in the else path (when conc is null), but the record is still created with those empty strings

The intent is defensive coding (create record even on null concordance), but a reader must trace through both branches to realize the defaults serve as the 'null concordance' case rather than being dead code
Extracting an extractHitData(conc) method returning a record with the 5 fields would make both branches explicit

issues.summary: All HTTP response objects are built as Map<String, Object>, making response shapes invisible to the type checker
issues.suggestion: Define response POJOs or records (e.g., SketchResponse, ExplorationResponse, ConcordanceResponse) and serialize them directly with JSON.toJSONString(). The model package classes (ExplorationResult, AdjectiveProfile) already have the right shape and could be the response objects with minor adjustments.
evidence: Every handler method constructs Map<String, Object> response = new HashMap<>() and calls response.put(key, value) — misspelled keys, wrong value types, or missing fields are all compile-time invisible

ExplorationHandlers.handleSemanticFieldExplore builds at least 10 response fields with keys like 'seed_collocates', 'discovered_nouns', 'core_collocates', 'edges' — no static check that these keys match what the frontend expects

SketchHandlers.handleBcqlQueryPost mixes conditional puts (if r.getRawXml() != null, if r.getCollocateLemma() != null) — the shape of the response object varies based on runtime state with no type expression
fastjson2 can serialize POJOs directly; the manual map-building adds type-unsafe indirection without any performance benefit

issues.summary: ConcordanceResult declares 7 fields as non-Optional String/primitive but documents them as nullable/zero, making the type system lie about their presence
issues.suggestion: Wrap nullable fields as Optional<String> (rawXml, lemma, tag, word, collocateLemma) or split ConcordanceResult into context-specific subtypes. At minimum, add @Nullable annotations and update callers to use Optional.ofNullable().ifPresent() patterns.
evidence: Fields rawXml, lemma, tag, word are String with no @Nullable annotation; Javadoc says they are null for some construction paths
collocateLemma is String but may be null; frequency and logDice are long/double but are 0/0.0 when collocateLemma is null, creating an implicit coupling between three fields
Callers in SketchHandlers check r.getRawXml() != null and r.getCollocateLemma() != null before using them — the type says String but runtime behavior requires null-checking

issues.summary: buildAndRankCollocates accepts a nullable Map<String, String> posMap but its signature does not express this
issues.suggestion: Change the parameter to accept an Optional<Map<String, String>> posMap or change it to Map<String, String> posMap with @Nullable annotation. Alternatively, make 'unknown' the default behavior explicit by providing an empty map: Collections.emptyMap() instead of null at call sites.
evidence: buildAndRankCollocates(Map<String, Long> freqMap, Map<String, String> posMap, ...) — posMap parameter has no @Nullable annotation
Called with null at multiple call sites: buildAndRankCollocates(freqMap, null, headwordFreq, ...) in executeSurfacePattern and findDependencyCollocations
Inside the method: String pos = posMap != null ? posMap.getOrDefault(collocateLemma, 'unknown') : 'unknown'; — the null guard is necessary but invisible from the call site

issues.summary: WordSketchResult.pos uses the string literal 'unknown' as a sentinel for missing POS, bypassing type-safe absence expression
issues.suggestion: Change WordSketchResult.pos to Optional<String> (or change the pos field to PosGroup rather than String). In buildAndRankCollocates, use null or Optional.empty() when posMap is absent, and let callers decide how to handle unknown POS rather than receiving a sentinel string.
evidence: buildAndRankCollocates: String pos = posMap != null ? posMap.getOrDefault(collocateLemma, "unknown") : "unknown"
WordSketchResult.getPos() returns String — callers cannot distinguish 'POS was not available' from a genuine POS tag value of 'unknown'
PosGroup enum exists and models POS categories correctly; WordSketchResult.pos bypasses it in favor of a raw String

issues.summary: RelationConfig record holds a nullable RelationType field without expressing this in the type
issues.suggestion: Change RelationConfig.relationType to Optional<RelationType> or add a non-null default (e.g., RelationType.SURFACE). Alternatively, throw IOException in parseRelationType for unrecognized values rather than returning null, which would catch config errors at load time rather than silently storing null.
evidence: RelationConfig record: RelationType relationType — typed as non-null but parseRelationType() returns null when value is absent or unrecognized
parseRelationType: catch (IllegalArgumentException e) { return null; } — returns null without documentation
SketchHandlers: rel.relationType() == RelationType.SURFACE — this null-unsafe comparison would throw NPE if relationType is null for a config entry

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor uses 'find' and 'execute' prefixes inconsistently for surface and dependency query methods at the same abstraction level
issues.suggestion: Standardize on 'find' prefix for all collocate-retrieval operations in the QueryExecutor interface: findCollocates(String lemma, String cqlPattern, ...) for surface patterns, findDependencyCollocates(String lemma, String deprel, ...) for dependency patterns. Reserve 'execute' for raw query execution methods that return concordance results (executeBcqlQuery, executeQuery already follow this pattern correctly).
evidence: findCollocations (interface) and executeSurfacePattern (interface) — both retrieve surface-pattern collocates but with different parameter signatures; 'find' vs 'execute' implies different abstraction levels but the methods are peers
findDependencyCollocations and executeDependencyPattern — same pattern: both query dependency relations, both return List<WordSketchResult>

In QueryExecutor interface, the Javadoc for executeSurfacePattern says 'Execute a surface pattern query' while findCollocations says 'Find collocations' — 'execute' connotes lower-level machinery, 'find' connotes higher-level intent, but semantically they are equivalent

callers in SemanticFieldExplorer use both: executor.executeSurfacePattern(...) for pattern-based lookup and executor.findCollocations(...) for reverse lookup — the naming does not help the caller choose

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti buries 'Multi' at the end; the multi-seed concept should lead the name
issues.suggestion: Rename to handleMultiSeedExploration (with its single-seed counterpart becoming handleSingleSeedExploration, or just handleSemanticFieldExploration). This puts the distinguishing concept first, matching how readers scan method names.
evidence: ExplorationHandlers methods: handleSemanticFieldExplore (single-seed) and handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti (multi-seed)
The discriminating concept (single vs. multi-seed) is the last word in a compound method name, requiring readers to read to the end before understanding which variant this is
The endpoint path is /api/semantic-field/explore-multi, which makes 'explore-multi' the unit — the Java method name inverts this to 'explore' + 'Multi' suffix

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.compare() does not communicate that it compares adjective/collocate profiles across seed nouns
issues.suggestion: Rename to compareCollocateProfiles(Set<String> nouns, ...) or profileNounCollocations(Set<String> nouns, ...). The ExplorationHandlers caller at handleSemanticField also calls it result = semanticFieldExplorer.compare(...) — renaming would make the handler intent clearer too.
evidence: Method signature: public ComparisonResult compare(Set<String> seedNouns, double minLogDice, int maxPerNoun)
'compare' is a generic verb that appears in Comparable.compareTo, Object.equals, and countless utility methods — it gives no information about what is being compared
The method compares adjective/collocate profiles across a set of seed nouns, a specific operation that could be named compareCollocateProfiles, compareNounCollocations, or profileNounCollocates

issues.summary: 'simplePattern' variable in ExplorationHandlers does not describe its role as a fallback pattern for reverse collocate lookup
issues.suggestion: Rename to reverseCollocatePattern or fallbackPosPattern to communicate that it is a POS-only pattern used for reverse lookup when the full BCQL pattern yields no results.
evidence: String simplePattern = relationConfig.get().collocatePosGroup() == PosGroup.ADJ ? "[xpos=\"JJ.*\"]": "[xpos=\"NN.*|VB.*\"]"
This pattern is then passed to SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern as its simplePattern parameter, which is used as a fallback when the BCQL pattern returns no results
'simple' is a relative adjective — simple compared to what? The variable's role is 'bare POS-only pattern used as reverse lookup fallback'

issues.summary: extractLemmaFromMatch and extractCollocateLemma in BlackLabSnippetParser have names implying different roles but implement the same operation
issues.suggestion: Remove extractLemmaFromMatch and consolidate callers onto extractCollocateLemma. If the head lemma extraction is needed, add extractHeadLemma that explicitly returns the FIRST match rather than the last. The existing extractHeadLemma() method (lines ~208–214) already exists as a one-liner delegating to extractLemmaAt — use that consistently.
evidence: extractLemmaFromMatch Javadoc: 'Finds all lemma="xxx" patterns and returns the last one (the collocate)' — the Javadoc itself says it returns 'the collocate', making it equivalent to extractCollocateLemma
extractCollocateLemma Javadoc: 'finds the last lemma attribute, which corresponds to the collocate in most patterns'
Both do: Matcher m = LEMMA_ATTR.matcher(matchText); String lastLemma = null; while (m.find()) { lastLemma = m.group(1); } return lastLemma;
The name 'extractLemmaFromMatch' sounds like it could return the head lemma, but it actually returns the last (collocate) lemma — the name is misleading

issues.summary: exploreByPattern takes a 'relationName' parameter used only for debug logging, obscuring that it has no effect on behavior
issues.suggestion: Either remove the relationName parameter and use a fixed log label or derive the name from bcqlPattern, or rename it to relationLabel or relationNameForLogging to signal that it is a display-only label with no behavioral effect.

relationName is used only in: logger.debug('Relation: {}', relationName) and logger.debug('Step 3: Scoring nouns by shared {}...', relationName) — purely for human-readable log messages

issues.summary: getTotalFrequency swallows exceptions and returns 0, silently corrupting logDice scores
issues.suggestion: Propagate the exception as IOException so callers can decide whether to skip or surface the error; alternatively, use a sentinel value (e.g. -1) with documented semantics and check it at all call sites.
evidence: catch (Exception e) { logger.warn(...); return 0; } in getTotalFrequency — index failures are indistinguishable from zero-frequency lemmas, making logDice silently incorrect for all callers

issues.summary: Sibling API route wrappers inconsistently catch RuntimeException — some routes will silently crash on unexpected errors
issues.suggestion: Standardise all route wrappers to catch both IOException and a top-level Exception with a 500 response, or extract a shared wrapHandler() utility that handles both cases uniformly.
evidence: /api/semantic-field/explore catches IOException + Exception; /api/semantic-field catches only IOException — an unexpected NullPointerException from handleSemanticField would propagate uncaught

issues.summary: handleBcqlQueryPost sends HTTP 400 for all exceptions including I/O failures that should be 500
issues.suggestion: Differentiate: catch InvalidQuery / IllegalArgumentException and send 400; catch IOException separately and send 500.
evidence: catch (Exception e) { logger.error(...); HttpApiUtils.sendError(exchange, 400, 'BCQL query error: ' + e.getMessage()); } — IOException from index access maps to 400 Bad Request, misleading clients

issues.summary: Main shutdown hook silently ignores IOException from executor.close()

evidence: Runtime.getRuntime().addShutdownHook(new Thread(() -> { ... try { executor.close(); } catch (IOException e) { // Ignore } }));

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer hosts result DTOs as inner classes while QueryResults holds sibling result types separately

issues.summary: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * contradicts the loopback-only security boundary for browser-based callers
issues.suggestion: Replace the wildcard with 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: http://localhost:3000' (or the specific webapp origin) if the intent is to serve only the bundled webapp; or document the wildcard as intentional if broad browser access is acceptable for a local development tool.
evidence: sendJsonResponse: exchange.getResponseHeaders().set("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "*");
sendError: exchange.getResponseHeaders().set("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "*");
sendBinaryResponse: exchange.getResponseHeaders().set("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "*");
Server binds to InetAddress.getLoopbackAddress() in WordSketchApiServer.start() — the network boundary is localhost, but CORS permits any browser origin to call the API

issues.summary: The 'intentionally unauthenticated, localhost-only' security posture is not documented anywhere in the code
issues.suggestion: Add a Javadoc comment to WordSketchApiServer noting 'This server is intentionally unauthenticated and binds to loopback only. Do not expose to a public network without adding authentication.' Also add a note to README.md.
